protocol.name: hyprland_toplevel_export_v1
protocol.copyright:

interface.name: hyprland_toplevel_export_manager_v1

description.summary: manager to create per-toplevel capture sources

interface.description: This object is a manager which offers requests to capture the contents of a toplevel window.

request.name: capture_toplevel
request.description: Capture the next frame of a toplevel identified by its XDG handle ID. Deprecated: use capture_toplevel_with_wlr_toplevel_handle instead.

request.name: destroy
request.description: All frame objects created by the manager remain valid until destroyed.

request.name: capture_toplevel_with_wlr_toplevel_handle
request.description: Capture the next frame of a toplevel identified by its wlr foreign toplevel handle. The compositor will send buffer format/size events followed by buffer_done, after which the client may call copy().

interface.name: hyprland_toplevel_export_frame_v1
interface.description: This object represents a single frame capture request for a toplevel. The compositor will send buffer announcements, then buffer_done. The client attaches a buffer and calls copy(). The compositor renders and sends flags + ready, or failed.

entry.name: already_used
entry.summary: copy was already requested on this object

event.name: buffer
event.description: Sent once after frame creation. Describes the wl_shm buffer format the client must use when calling copy().

event.name: damage
event.description: May be sent zero or more times before ready when ignore_damage was not set in the copy request. Reports a bounding box of changed pixels since the last successful capture. For advisory use only.

event.name: flags
event.description: Sent once just before ready. Carries per-frame flags.

event.name: ready
event.description: Sent when the frame has been rendered into the attached buffer. The timestamp reflects the compositor clock at capture time.

event.name: failed
event.description: The capture could not be completed. The client must not call copy() again on this object.

event.name: linux_dmabuf
event.description: Sent once after frame creation (alongside buffer). Describes an alternative DMA-BUF format the client may use.

event.name: buffer_done
event.description: Sent after all buffer / linux_dmabuf events. The client may now create a buffer and call copy().

request.name: copy
request.description: Attach a buffer and request that the compositor render the toplevel into it. The buffer must match the format/size announced in buffer() or linux_dmabuf(). On success the compositor sends flags + ready; on failure it sends failed.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroys the frame. Safe to call at any time before or after copy.
